task_completion.description: Open the Preferences or Settings menu in the Avare app
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the Avare app's Preferences or Settings menu.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Aircraft performance settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the specific sub-menu or dialog for Aircraft, Performance, or Profile settings within Avare.

task_completion.description: Set the Fuel Burn Rate to 10
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of an input field labeled 'Fuel Burn', 'Fuel Burn Rate', or similar, containing the exact value '10' or '10.0'.

task_completion.description: Set the Planned True Airspeed (TAS) to 115
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of an input field labeled 'TAS', 'True Airspeed', 'Planned TAS', or similar, containing the exact value '115'.

task_completion.description: Return to the main map view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshot must show the Avare main map interface, typically displaying aviation charts, a moving map, or GPS navigation view.

integrity.description: Agent used the Avare app GUI to configure settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show standard Android UI interactions within the Avare app, without any terminal, command prompt, or ADB shell windows visible.

integrity.description: Agent entered values into the actual settings fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The values '10' and '115' must be visible inside the correct preference input dialogs, not typed into a generic text editor, search bar, or unrelated app.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a script or shortcut to bypass navigation
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a logical progression of screenshots showing navigation from the main map, to settings, to aircraft profile, and back to the map.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring the Avare Aviation GPS app (package: com.ds.avare). The target Fuel Burn Rate must be set to 10 (or 10.0) GPH. The target Planned True Airspeed (TAS) must be set to 115 knots. These values correspond to a Piper PA-28-180 Cherokee.
